problem.title: Power of Three
problem.description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of three. Otherwise, return false. An integer n is a power of three if there exists an integer x such that n == 3^x. Try to solve it without using loops or recursion.
assumptions: n can be negative or zero (return false).

example.explanation: 27 = 3^3. Without a loop: the largest power of 3 that fits in an int is 3^19 = 1162261467. If n > 0 and 1162261467 % n == 0, then n is a power of 3 (since 3^19 only has 3 as a prime factor).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Max Power Divisibility [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: The largest power of 3 within 32-bit integers is 3^19 = 1162261467. Since 3 is prime, any power of 3 must divide 3^19 exactly. So n is a power of 3 if and only if n > 0 and 1162261467 % n == 0. This is O(1) with no loop.
solutions.code_python:
def is_power_of_three(n: int) -> bool:
    return n > 0 and 1162261467 % n == 0

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Iterative Division
solutions.description: Repeatedly divide n by 3 while divisible. If n reduces to 1, it was a power of 3.
